title: Lacmellea floribunda
taxonomy: Lacmellea floribunda is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is a species of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Apocynaceae family, Lacmellea floribunda is placed in the subfamily Rauvoloideae. It is a part of the tribe Landopsideae. Lacmellea floribunda is further categorized as a member of the genus Lacmellea.

intro: Lacmellea floribunda is a perennial climber in the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. Bioactive constituents of Lacmellea floribunda include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lacmellea floribunda is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
